data.description:
## Why

Non-structural findings (coherence and other plan-semantics criteria) re-litigate closed children
too, and carry no `_container_child`. Without a finding<->closed-child association we cannot tell a
live signal from noise, and cannot distinguish plan-semantics from delivered-functionality.

## What

Add a SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call `plan_review_completion` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py`,
mirroring the `plan_review_novelty` sub-call (its own contract + prompt), that ALONE receives the
delivered-children manifest (their own AC text) and emits, per finding, three atomic sub-answers:
ATTRIBUTION (closed child id or none; structural pass-through for G3/G4 `_container_child`),
CONTAINMENT (`limited-to-closed | spans-open-or-system | n/a`), LAYER
(`plan-semantics | delivered-functionality | n/a`). Write the rubric prompt at
`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_completion.md`, register the contract, and add it to the
derived prompt index. Bounded/validated outputs; every ambiguous value fails toward keep. Frozen
wording (not regenerated per run) per G-Eval guidance.

## Scope

- IN: the sub-call + contract registration + the prompt + output parsing/validation + prompt-index
  entry.
- OUT: the Pass-3 drop decision (separate story); the gold-set calibration (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `plan_review_completion` sub-call exists, registered like `plan_review_novelty`, receiving ONLY the delivered-children manifest (Pass-1 independence preserved).
- [ ] Per finding it emits {attribution, containment, layer} with the closed enums above; malformed/insufficient answers coerce to the keep-safe value.
- [ ] G3/G4 attribution is taken structurally from `_container_child` (not re-asked); a None/unattributed finding is never force-attributed.
- [ ] The rubric prompt `plan_review_completion.md` exists, is in the prompt index, and passes the CI drift gate; wording is frozen.
- [ ] Unit tests cover parsing + the fail-safe coercions for each sub-answer.
data.title: plan_review_completion Pass-2 sub-call: attribution + containment + layer sub-questions
